bones.name: Humerus
description: Articulates at proximal end with scapula through shoulder joint
Articulates at distal end with radius and ulna at elbow joint

bones.name: Head
description: Smooth articular surface covered with hyaline cartilage.
Articulates with Glenoid fossa of scapula.

bones.name: Greater tubercle
description: A large, raised protrusion on the lateral surface of proximal end.
The posterior surface provides attachment for the supraspinatus, infraspinatus, and teres minor muscles from superior to inferior respectively.
Inferior portion forms the crest of the greater tubercle and provides attachment for the pectoralis major muscle.

bones.name: Lesser tubercle
description: A protrusion that lies on anterior surface of proximal end.
Insertion site for the subscapularis muscle.
Inferior portion forms the crest of the lesser tubercle and is insertion site for the teres major muscle.

bones.name: Intertubercular sulcus
description: A narrow depression that lies between the greater and lesser tubercles.
Insertion site for the latissumus dorsi muscle.
The long head of the biceps brachii tendon passes through this sulcus.

bones.name: Anatomical neck
description: is constriction located just inferior and lateral to the head.
It indicates the line of circumference for capsule of shoulder joint.
indicates the most proximal portion of the shaft.
Fractures here may damage the axillary nerve and the posterior circumflex humoral artery.

bones.name: Shaft
description: Mostly cylindrical in the proximal half but is triangular in cross section in the distal portion.
The posterior surface provides origins of the lateral and medial heads of triceps brachii muscle.
The anterior surface provides insertion of the coracobrachialis muscle as well as the origin of the brachialis and brachioradialis muscles.

bones.name: Deltoid tuberosity
description: A roughened process on the lateral surface.
Provides insertion for the deltoid muscle.

bones.name: Radial sulcus
description: A spiraled groove on the posterior surface of the
The radial nerve and the deep brachial vessels travel in this depression.
Fractures of the shaft can damage these structures.

bones.name: Condyles
description: Begin as widening of the shaft on medial and lateral surfaces.
Lateral epicondyle lies proximal to the
and provides origin for the anconeus and superficial head of the supinator muscles as well as the common extensor tendon*.
Medial epicondyle lies proximal to the
and provides origin for the pronator teres muscle as well as the common flexor tendon**.
*Common extensor tendon: Extensor carpi radialis brevis, extensor digitorum, extensor digiti minimi, and extensor carpi ulnaris.
**Common flexor tendon: flexor carpi radialis, palmaris longus, flexor carpi ulnaris, and flexor digitorum superficialis.

bones.name: Radial and Coronoid fossae
description: is a shallow depression on the medial side of the anterior surface just superior to the
of the radius when the forearm is flexed at the elbow.
is a shallow depression on the lateral side of the anterior surface just superior to the
of the ulna when the forearm is flexed at the elbow.

bones.name: Olecranon fossa
description: A deep depression on posterior surface.
Lies just distal to the trochlea.
of the ulna when the forearm is extended at the elbow.

bones.name: Capitulum and Trochlea
description: is a rounded projection on the anterior and inferior surface of distal end of the humerus.
Articulates with the proximal surface of the
is a saddle-like process at distal end of the humerus and extends onto the posterior surface of the bone.
Articulates with the trochlear notch of the ulna.
* The ulnar nerve travels in a small sulcus between the trochlea and medial condyle

bones.name: Ulna
description: Bone on medial side of forearm.
Articulates with radius at proximal and distal ends.
Articulates with carpal bones of hand at distal end through the articular disc.

bones.name: Olecranon process
description: A projection at the proximal end of the ulna that curves anteriorly.
The anterior surface has a tip that is shaped like a beak and enters the
of humerus when forearm is extended at the elbow.
Posterior surface provides attachment for the insertions of triceps brachii and anconeus muscles.
Can be palpated as the “point of the elbow” on its proximal and posterior surface.

bones.name: Trochlear notch
description: A curved articular surface that lies between anterior part of olecranon process proximally and coronoid process distally.

bones.name: Coronoid process
description: A beak-like projection that is located anteriorly at the proximal end.
of the humerus when the forearm is flexed at the elbow.
Ulnar head of the pronator teres muscle originates from medial surface of the process.

bones.name: Ulnar tuberosity
description: A rough protrusion on anterior surface just distal to the coronoid process.
Provides the insertion for the brachialis muscle.

bones.name: Radial notch
description: A concave depression that is located just lateral to the coronoid process on the anterior surface of ulna.
Articulates with the discoid surface of the

bones.name: Styloid process
description: A protrusion that extends distally from the posteromedial aspect of the ulna.

bones.name: Radius
description: Bone on lateral side of forearm
Articulates with ulna at proximal and distal ends
of the humerus at proximal end.
Articulates with the carpal bones of the hand at the distal end.
During pronation, the radius will pivot on its long axis and cross the ulna.

bones.name: Head
description: The most proximal end of the radius is smooth and discoid in shape.
The proximal surface is concaved and articulates with the
The smooth circumference of the disc articulates with the ulna at the
and is covered by the annular ligament.
Both articular surfaces are covered by hyaline cartilage.

bones.name: Neck
description: A constriction that is located just distal to the head.

bones.name: Radial tuberosity
description: A rough protrusion on the medial surface and is located just below the neck.
Provides the insertion for the biceps brachii muscle.

bones.name: Ulnar notch
description: A concave depression on medial surface of the distal end.
Articulates with the head of the ulna.

bones.name: Styloid process
description: A rough protrusion on the lateral surface that projects distally past the end of the radius.
The brachioradialis muscle inserts on the proximal part of the process.
It can be palpated when the tendons around it are slack.

bones.name: Dorsal tubercle
description: A rough protuberance located on the posterior surface of the distal end of the radius.
Acts as a fulcrum for the tendon of the extensor pollicis longus tendon.

bones.name: Hand
description: The hand is composed of 27 bones
The carpal bones are arranged in two rows.
The proximal row includes the scaphoid, lunate, triquetrum, and pisiform.
The distal row includes the trapezium, trapezoid, capitate, and hamate.
The scaphoid, lunate, and triquetrum bones articulate with the radius and articular disc of the radio-ulnar joint.
* A mnemonic for remembering the carpal bones is “Scary Lunatics Try Poisons That They Can’t Handle.
The first letter of each word is the first letter of each of the bones from lateral to medial, proximal row first.

bones.name: Scaphoid
description: The largest bone in the proximal row.
Its is the most frequently fractured carpal bone – usually as result of a fall onto an outstretched (hyperextended) hand.
Has a prominent tubercle on its lateral palmar surface.

bones.name: Lunate
description: A semilunar-shaped bone located between the scaphoid and triquetrum bones in the proximal row.
Distally, it articulates with the capitate and hamate.

bones.name: Triquetrum
description: Articulates with the hamate and the lunate bones.
Is the only bone that articulates with the pisiform bone.

bones.name: Pisiform
description: A small bone in the proximal row shaped like a pea.
Acts as a sesamoid bone for the tendon of the flexor carpi ulnaris muscle.

bones.name: Trapezium
description: The most lateral carpal bone of the distal row.
Forms a saddle joint with the first metacarpal bone (thumb).
Palmar surface has a tubercle that attaches to the flexor retinaculum.

bones.name: Trapezoid
description: A small, irregular bone named for its trapezoid shape.

bones.name: Capitate
description: Articulates with the second, third and fourth metacarpal bones distally and the scaphoid and lunate bones proximally.
Articulates with the hamate medially and the trapezoid laterally.
Part of the oblique head of the adductor pollicis muscle originates on the palmar surface.

bones.name: Hamate
description: An irregular shaped bone with a hamulus (hook) on its palmar surface.
Hamulus is attached to flexor retinaculum.

bones.name: Metacarpals
description: Numbered from one to five from lateral to medial.
Each has a base, shaft, and head from proximal to distal.
First metacarpal (thumb) is convex on its proximal surface and articulates with trapezium to form a saddle joint.

bones.name: Text
description: Each finger has a proximal, middle, and distal phalanx except
The thumb only has a proximal and distal phalanx.
Each phalanx has a base, shaft, and head from proximal to distal.
